raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and distinction from prior routing methods.","R1.2":"The delegation makes logical consistency load-bearing because the artifact contains an apparent contradiction between a buffer-free method and a proof using a validation buffer.","R1.3":"Improvement centrally includes checking that reported empirical claims and design conclusions are aligned with the stated results.","R1.5":"A draft-level improvement reasonably includes section-level narrative ordering and how theory, method, and experiments support the contribution.","R1.6":"The broad request to improve the draft makes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and register load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any projection of draft improvement must account for uncertainty about unstated author intent and unavailable external context.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong causal and comparative claims, so avoiding unsupported overclaims is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within the requested draft-improvement responsibility rather than performing unrelated review or execution.","RX.4":"Improvement should avoid introducing misleading scientific claims or changes that could harm downstream readers' understanding.","RX.5":"Traceability is needed so any projected responsibility remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact text."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and distinction from prior routing methods.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation makes logical consistency load-bearing because the artifact contains an apparent contradiction between a buffer-free method and a proof using a validation buffer.
rationale.R1.3: Improvement centrally includes checking that reported empirical claims and design conclusions are aligned with the stated results.
rationale.R1.5: A draft-level improvement reasonably includes section-level narrative ordering and how theory, method, and experiments support the contribution.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request to improve the draft makes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and register load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of draft improvement must account for uncertainty about unstated author intent and unavailable external context.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong causal and comparative claims, so avoiding unsupported overclaims is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within the requested draft-improvement responsibility rather than performing unrelated review or execution.
rationale.RX.4: Improvement should avoid introducing misleading scientific claims or changes that could harm downstream readers' understanding.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is needed so any projected responsibility remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact text.
